channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Pakistan bus crash kills 40
item.description: At least 40 people were killed and eight others injured after a passenger bus plunged into a ravine in Pakistan.

item.title: World leaders pay tribute to Iran’s late leader Khamenei in Tehran
item.description: World leaders pay tribute to Iran's late leader Khamenei in Tehran

item.title: Heavy rain floods Mumbai during intense monsoon spell
item.description: Heavy monsoon rains have inundated parts of Mumbai, India's financial capital, causing widespread flooding.

item.title: Germany’s Merz defends NATO spending after Trump calls it ‘ridiculous’
item.description: Back and forth over defence spending comes as NATO leaders set to meet in Ankara next week.

item.title: Hottest June on record in France as heatwave deaths rise to alarming levels
item.description: The average temperatures on June 24 and 25 this year were the highest ever recorded in the country.

item.title: Yemen’s Houthis threaten Saudi Arabia after alleged airspace intrusion
item.description: Army says it confronted Saudi planes that ⁠⁠allegedly tried to ⁠⁠stop Iranian civilian ​aircraft from landing in Sanaa.

item.title: Why is the UK mired in a maternity and neonatal deaths scandal?
item.description: An inquiry found that more than 500 mothers and babies either came to harm or died due to poor care.

item.title: Gaza sports community pledges to continue legacy of killed goalkeeper
item.description: Slain seeking cooking gas, Gaza goalkeeper Saleem al-Ashqar joins more than 1,000 Palestinian athletes killed in war.

item.title: Mourners bury victims of Damascus cafe bombing with six lawyers among dead
item.description: Syrian Bar Association mourns six lawyers amid international condemnation of Thursday's attack in Damascus.

item.title: Election wins prove pro-Palestine US campus protests didn’t fail: Activists
item.description: With Columbia University set to be represented in Congress by former protester, advocates see 'new wave of hope'.

item.title: Osaka defeats Kasatkina to make Wimbledon round of 16
item.description: Four-time grand slam winner continues to impress at SW19, losing only four games to Daria ⁠Kasatkina in round three.

item.title: Russia’s advance collapses in Ukraine, ‘40,000’ troops killed in June
item.description: Ukraine's long-range strikes hit targets in Russia, where anxiety is rising.

item.title: Interpol names Ukrainian woman as suspect in Monaco bombing
item.description: Anastasiia Berezovska is named as the chief suspect responsible for injuring Ukrainian tycoon Vadym Yermolaiev.

item.title: Canada vs Morocco: World Cup Round of 16: Saibari, prediction, schedule
item.description: World Cup cohosts Canada face mighty Morocco in their first-ever last-16 appearance at the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Video: The Cape Verde effect
item.description: Cape Verde's World Cup run has become about more than football.

item.title: UN sounds ‘red alert’ over human rights catastrophe in Sudan’s el-Obeid
item.description: UN human rights chief Volker Turk issued the warning over growing fears of an imminent RSF assault.

item.title: World cup wrap day 22
item.description: World cup wrap day 22

item.title: Don’t eat your greens? A diet taking Yemen by storm has doctors worried
item.description: Hospitals in Yemen report increase in workload after struck off doctor encourages diabetes patients not to take insulin.

item.title: ‘If it dies, it’s on you’: Saving Nigeria’s Benin bronze casting
item.description: Behind the global restitution story, master artisans are fighting to keep a centuries-old craft alive.

item.title: Argentina fans flood Miami for Cape Verde match
item.description: Argentina fans flood Miami for Cape Verde match

item.title: Mourners pay respects as Iran’s slain leader Ali Khamenei lies in state
item.description: Iran begins seven-day state funeral for late ayatollah as some 100 foreign delegations arrive under a tense ceasefire

item.title: Video: Ronaldo appears to say ‘Bismillah’ before penalty
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo appeared to say ‘Bismillah’ (In the name of God) twice before taking a penalty.

item.title: Bangkok’s ambulance volunteers: Saving lives or putting them at risk?
item.description: 101 East enters the dangerous world of Bangkok's volunteer ambulance crews racing to be first at accident scenes.

item.title: An AIDS-free generation is within reach, but not guaranteed
item.description: New tools and community-led care can save lives, but only if governments sustain the global HIV response.

item.title: Argentina fans revel in Messi’s Miami homecoming before Cape Verde match
item.description: Argentinian supporters turn Miami Beach into a celebratory hub ahead of their team's only World Cup match in Messi city.
